arabic: يَٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓا۟ أَطِيعُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَأَطِيعُوا۟ ٱلرَّسُولَ وَأُو۟لِى ٱلْأَمْرِ مِنكُمْ
translation: “O you who believe, obey God, obey the Messenger, and those in authority among you.”

sunni.label: Sunni emphasis
sunni.body: Sunni tradition reads it as the community's rulers and scholars — their obedience is conditional, and disputes return to God and the Messenger.

shia.label: Shia emphasis
shia.body: Shia tradition reads those in authority as the infallible Imams — their obedience is joined to God's without condition, so they must be divinely appointed.

voiceover.hook: One phrase in this verse has been weighed for a thousand years — and two traditions weigh it differently.
voiceover.question: Which of these two readings resonates with you

cta_text: Which of these two readings resonates with you?
caption_desc: Ūlū al-Amr — one verse, two readings 🌙 #quran #tafsir #shia #sunni
